labels.note: Standard parties and premises clause.
Standard fixed-term clause.
Standard rent amount and due date.
A last-month deposit of one month's rent is expressly permitted.
Damage/security deposits are not permitted under the RTA.
Pet deposits (and non-refundable deposits generally) are not permitted.
A tenancy agreement cannot prohibit pets; no-pet clauses are void.
Refundable key deposit at replacement cost is permitted.
Landlord cannot require permission for guests or charge guest fees.
Entry requires 24 hours' written notice except in emergencies or with consent.
Standard tenant cleanliness obligation.
Standard tenant damage responsibility, consistent with the RTA.
The NSF administration charge cannot exceed 20 dollars.
A tenant cannot be required to provide post-dated cheques.
Rent increases require 90 days' notice, once per 12 months, capped by the guideline.
The landlord must pay annual interest on the rent deposit.
Contents insurance is the tenant's choice; a standard clause.
An absolute ban is void; the landlord cannot unreasonably withhold consent.
Standard landlord maintenance obligation, consistent with the RTA.
A landlord cannot withhold or shut off vital services.
A landlord cannot change the locks without giving the tenant new keys.
Landlord and tenant may agree to prohibit smoking in the unit.
Assigning utility responsibility to the tenant is permitted if specified.
A standard severability clause; consistent with the Act.
